description:
You are a configuration-management engineer who needs to provide a quick report of every permission-related change that occurred today.  
    A colleague has already placed the raw audit file at  

        /home/user/config_changes.log

    The log is plain-text and each event is contained on one line in the following fixed layout (all fields separated by a single space, no extraneous spaces, each line ends with a Unix newline, “\n”):

        YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS HOST=<hostname> USER=<user> ACTION=<action> TYPE=<type> [additional-fields...]

    Your task:

    1. Read /home/user/config_changes.log.  
    2. Extract only the lines whose TYPE field is exactly “TYPE=PERMISSION”.  
       • Use a regular expression so that the filter will still work if the date, host, or user strings differ in the future.  
    3. Write the matching lines—in their original order and 100 % unmodified—to a new file named  

        /home/user/permission_changes.log

    Output file formatting requirements that the automated checker will verify:  
    • The file must contain ONLY the matching lines, nothing else (no header line, no blank lines).  
    • Lines must appear in the exact same sequence as they were found in /home/user/config_changes.log.  
    • The file must end with a single trailing newline (i.e., the last line is terminated by “\n”).  
    • File permissions should be the default ones created by your shell (do NOT attempt to chmod the new file).

    Notes / hints (not mandatory steps):  
    • A simple single grep-like command with an appropriate regex is enough.  
    • You do not need sudo privileges.  
    • Do not delete or alter the original log.

    When you are done, you can cat /home/user/permission_changes.log to show the result—but remember that only the existence and exact contents of the file will be graded.
truth:
# Pre-existing file
    Path: /home/user/config_changes.log
    Contents (including required final trailing newline):

2024-06-01 10:15:23 HOST=web01 USER=root ACTION=CREATE TYPE=FILE PATH=/etc/nginx/nginx.conf
2024-06-01 10:17:05 HOST=web01 USER=root ACTION=MODIFY TYPE=PERMISSION CMD=chmod 644 /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
2024-06-01 10:20:10 HOST=db01 USER=admin ACTION=MODIFY TYPE=CONTENT CMD=vim /etc/mysql/my.cnf
2024-06-01 10:21:22 HOST=web01 USER=root ACTION=MODIFY TYPE=PERMISSION CMD=chown root:root /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
2024-06-01 10:23:45 HOST=web01 USER=root ACTION=DELETE TYPE=FILE PATH=/tmp/test.tmp
2024-06-01 10:25:50 HOST=db01 USER=admin ACTION=MODIFY TYPE=PERMISSION CMD=chmod 600 /etc/mysql/my.cnf

    # Expected artefact created by the agent
    Path: /home/user/permission_changes.log
    Expected contents (including required final trailing newline):

2024-06-01 10:17:05 HOST=web01 USER=root ACTION=MODIFY TYPE=PERMISSION CMD=chmod 644 /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
2024-06-01 10:21:22 HOST=web01 USER=root ACTION=MODIFY TYPE=PERMISSION CMD=chown root:root /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
2024-06-01 10:25:50 HOST=db01 USER=admin ACTION=MODIFY TYPE=PERMISSION CMD=chmod 600 /etc/mysql/my.cnf

    # Verification steps performed by the automated grader
    1. Confirm that /home/user/permission_changes.log exists.
    2. Check that its SHA-256 hash equals the hash of the three permission lines shown above (with final newline).
    3. Verify that the file contains exactly three lines and each line appears in the same order as in the source log.
name: task_000000_16b1f4a8
